title: Add DefaultContent API to retrieve apparmor profile content
body:
The default apparmor profile is not stored on disk which causes
confusion when debugging the content of the profile. To solve this, we
now add an additional API which returns the profile as byte slice.

comments.body: Should this be part of the make install and crio config?

> Should this be part of the make install and crio config?

I'm unsure and I see the point that it is not nice to write to `/etc`. We could write the file to somewhere else but then we would have to log the profile location in API consumers like CRI-O.

I generally like the idea having it in memory, but we have to tell the customers somehow what the content of the profile is... @vrothberg WDYT?

If it's only for dumping the profile at some point somewhere, I would prefer to add a dedicated function `DumpDefaultProfileToPath(path string) error` or something similar - it could also just return `[]bytes` in case we want to log the entire content. This way, consumers of the package have full control. It's utterly important to keep the in-memory processing as is. It had significant impact on performance.

> If it's only for dumping the profile at some point somewhere, I would prefer to add a dedicated function `DumpDefaultProfileToPath(path string) error` or something similar - it could also just return `[]bytes` in case we want to log the entire content. This way, consumers of the package have full control. It's utterly important to keep the in-memory processing as is. It had significant impact on performance.

Sounds good, I changed the implementation to add an additional API endpoint.

LGTM
